DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances VIA Zoom Conference
Elizabeth R. Malay, Esq., COLLIER SOCKS LLP (415) 767-0047 liz@colliersocks.com FOR Plaintiff ANN WEST
Dustin L. Collier, Esq., COLLIER SOCKS LLP (415) 767-0047 dustin@colliersocks.com FOR Plaintiff ANN WEST (via ZOOM)
Dylan Denio, Esq. / Karen L. Alexander, Esq., DUANE MORRIS LLP +1 619 744 2200 ddenio@duanemorris.com / klalexander@duanemorris.com Attorneys for Defendant
MANOUSO MANOS

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MANOUSO MANOS DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

Defendant Manos's demurrer to cause of five [Bane Act] in the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is OVERRULED.

A demurrer tests the legal sufficiency of the complaint. (CCP 430.10(e); Rakestraw v. California Physicians' Serv. (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 39, 42-43.) To withstand a demurrer, a plaintiff must show that the complaint alleges facts sufficient to establish every element of each cause of action. (Id. at p. 43.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) The court liberally construes the SAC per CCP 452.

The demurrer to cause of action five [Bane Act] is OVERRULED. "Civil Code section 52.1 requires an attempted or completed act of interference with a legal right, accompanied by a form of coercion." (Austin B. v. Escondido Union School Dist. (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 860, 882 (cleaned up).) "The essence of a Bane Act claim is that the defendant, by the specified improper means (i.e., 'threat[], intimidation or coercion'), tried to or did prevent the plaintiff from doing something he or she had the right to do under the law or to force the plaintiff to do something that he or she was not required to do under the law." (Austin B., 149 Cal.App.4th at 883; CACI 3066.) Here, plaintiff adequately alleges that defendant interfered with plaintiff's right to be free from and compensated for sexual harassment (Civil Code section 51.9) by threatening to and ultimately shaming and exiling plaintiff from the Iyenger yoga community. (SAC, pars. 97-98.)

Order submitted.

Judge: Victor Hwang; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/VMH)

Hearing is held. Argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANN WEST's Motion To Stay Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 918.5.

Plaintiff's motion to stay per CCP 918.5 is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. CCP 918.5(a) gives the trial court the discretion to "stay the enforcement of a judgment or order." One factor in exercising its discretion is the amount of the creditor's judgment or order. (CCP 918.5(b)(2).) Here, there is presently no fee order for the court to stay and the amount of fees is therefore unknown. This motion is premature.

Order submitted.
